{"step_by_step_thinking": "Standardization is a statistical method used to compare the rates or proportions of different populations. Direct standardization uses the age-specific rates of the study population and applies them to the age distribution of the standard population, while indirect standardization uses the age-specific rates of the standard population and applies them to the study population. The national population is not always taken as the standard population. Therefore, statement B is false.", "answer_choice": "B"}
